description: Multi-state part-year fixture: Single filer moves PA -> IL mid-year TY2025. PA resident Jan 1 - Jun 30 (181 days), IL resident Jul 1 - Dec 31 (184 days). Two W-2s: Philly Corp ($30k, $2,500 fed wh, $921 PA wh) and Chicago LLC ($35k, $4,000 fed wh, $1,732.50 IL wh). Standard deduction, no dependents, no OBBBA triggers, no itemized, no Schedule C/E. This is the FIRST golden fixture that exercises the state plugin dispatch layer end-to-end (federal compute -> registry.get("PA")/get("IL") -> per-state StateReturn).
authority: skill/scripts/states/pa.py — PA flat 3.07% on tenforty PA-40 base; v1 nonresident/part-year uses days_in_state/365 proration of resident-basis tax

skill/scripts/states/il.py — IL flat 4.95% on (federal AGI + Sch M adds - Sch M subs) - exemption; TY2025 personal exemption $2,850; v1 nonresident/part-year uses days_in_state/365 proration of resident-basis tax

skill/reference/state-reciprocity.json — PA reciprocity partners {IN, MD, NJ, OH, VA, WV}; IL reciprocity partners {IA, KY, MI, WI}; PA<->IL is NOT reciprocal so both states tax the income earned while a resident
test_golden_simple_w2.py locks Single $65k W-2 -> $5,755 federal income tax for TY2025 (same federal totals as this fixture: $65k AGI, $15,750 std ded, $49,250 TI)

federal_hand_check.agi_calc: no adjustments to income -> AGI = total_income = 65000
federal_hand_check.federal_income_tax: Locked to simple_w2_standard golden: IRS tax tables Single TY2025 -> 5755 at TI 49250 (10% * 11925 + 12% * 36550 + 22% * 800 = 5754.50 -> 5755 via $50 tax-table midpoint rounding)

pa_hand_check.pa_compensation_base: PA's PA-40 starts from 8 income classes; for a wage-only filer, class 1 (compensation) = $65,000 W-2 sum (BOTH W-2s, because tenforty marshals the canonical w2_income sum and PA does not separate by state row at this layer)

il_hand_check.il_base_income_calc: federal AGI 65000 + Sch M additions 0 - Sch M subtractions 0 = 65000
il_hand_check.il_additions_detail: 0 — no 1099-INT box 8, no 1099-DIV box 11 (no muni interest)
il_hand_check.il_subtractions_detail: 0 — no SSA-1099 box 5, no 1099-R box 2a, no 1099-INT box 3 (no Treasury interest)

TODO_real_sourcing_logic.scope: The v1 PA and IL plugins use a flat days_in_state/365 proration of the RESIDENT-BASIS tax. The brief's idealized hand-check assumed cleanly-separable income sourcing (PA tax = $30k * 3.07% = $921; IL tax = ($35k - $2,850) * 4.95% = $1,591.43) — but the v1 plugins do NOT implement that. They compute the tax as if the taxpayer were a full-year resident of the state on the entire $65k of wages, then prorate. This OVER-states tax on both sides for a clean PA->IL move because each state ends up taxing a fraction of the OTHER state's wages.
TODO_real_sourcing_logic.pa_real_logic_should: PA Schedule NRH-NR sources class 1 (compensation) to the work location. A clean PA->IL mover with the Philly job ending Jun 30 should owe PA tax on only the $30,000 Philly wages = $30,000 * 0.0307 = $921.00 exactly (zero refund / zero owed because withholding matches). The v1 plugin returns $989.55 owed instead — a $68.55 over-statement and a $68.55 false 'owed' position.
TODO_real_sourcing_logic.il_real_logic_should: IL Schedule NR sources wages to work location. A clean PA->IL mover with the Chicago job starting Jul 1 should owe IL tax on only the $35,000 Chicago wages, less the $2,850 personal exemption = $32,150 * 0.0495 = $1,591.43 exactly. With $1,732.50 withheld, the real refund would be $141.07. The v1 plugin returns $1,550.86 owed and a $181.64 refund — a $40.57 under-statement of tax and a $40.57 over-statement of refund.
TODO_real_sourcing_logic.fan_out_followup_pa: Implement PA Schedule NRH (Apportionment of Business, Profession, or Farm Income) plus class-1 sourcing on PA-40 line 1a using W-2 state_rows[].state == 'PA' and state_wages instead of the canonical box1_wages sum.
TODO_real_sourcing_logic.fan_out_followup_il: Implement IL Schedule NR step-by-step: source wages to W-2 state_rows[].state == 'IL', source interest/dividends to domicile (full Sch B amounts allocated by residency dates), and feed the IL-source AGI into the IL-1040 instead of federal AGI.
TODO_real_sourcing_logic.lock_intent: This fixture LOCKS the v1 day-prorated outputs so any future change to the plugins (including the eventual real sourcing logic implementation) is detected as a regression and surfaced for explicit re-blessing. When real sourcing lands, this fixture's expected_pa_state_return and expected_il_state_return blocks must be updated to the brief's idealized hand-check values: PA $921.00 / IL $1,591.43.

TODO_il_adds_subs_no_op.note: CP8-D added IL-1040 Line 2 muni-interest addback, IL-1040 Line 5 Social Security + qualified retirement subtraction, and IL Schedule M Step 2 Line 22 U.S. Treasury subtraction. This fixture has ZERO 1099-INT, ZERO 1099-DIV, ZERO SSA-1099, and ZERO 1099-R, so all CP8-D adds/subs evaluate to 0 and the IL base income equals federal AGI. Verify by reading expected_il_state_return.state_specific.il_additions_total and il_subtractions_total — both must be '0.00'. A future fixture should exercise the CP8-D adds/subs with non-zero muni and Treasury interest.
